vulnerability.title: Enable Randomized Layout of Virtual Address Space
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the kernel.randomize_va_space kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w kernel.randomize_va_space=2 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: kernel.randomize_va_space = 2
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of kernel.randomize_va_space from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*kernel.randomize_va_space.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "kernel.randomize_va_space" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi done # # Set sysctl config f

triage_decision.reason: Disabling or enabling kernel address space randomization is a low-impact sysctl tweak that can be applied persistently without risking loss of console or SSH access. No system services are directly dependent on the setting, and any changes can be rolled back easily.

execution_details.stdout:
# when using 1 interface for two networks when using NETKEY, the kernel
# thinks it can be clever by sending a redirect (cause it cannot tell
# an encrypted packet came in, but a decrypted packet came out),
# so it sends a bogus ICMP redirect
#
# We disable redirects for XFRM/IPsec
# net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0

review_feedback: Review+QA approved but the verification scan failed. Try a different approach.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation correctly persists kernel.randomize_va_space=2 by removing existing lines and adding a dedicated config file. It generally resolves the issue but could be streamlined for better idempotency and safety.
concerns: Backup of entire /etc/sysctl.d directory may be unnecessary and creates large backup copies.
Regex removal may not handle all whitespace or comment variations in sysctl files.
No verification that the new value is already set before writing the file.
Potential conflict if another sysctl file later overrides the setting.
The script does not check for existence of target files before modifying them, risking errors on systems without /etc/sysctl.d.
suggested_improvements: Use a more precise pattern (e.g., ^kernel\.randomize_va_space[[:space:]]*=) to match only assignments and ignore comments.
Add a check to confirm the current value is not already 2 before overwriting.
Write the new file atomically (e.g., using a temporary file and mv) to avoid partial writes.
Include idempotent handling for repeated execution (skip if already set).
Validate that /etc/sysctl.d exists and is writable before proceeding.
Consider naming the file with a higher prefix (e.g., 99-...) to ensure precedence over default files.
